message:
perf: preserve string buffer capacity by replacing mem::take with clone+clear

mem::take moves the buffer's heap allocation to the consumer, forcing a
fresh allocation on the next loop iteration. Using clone()+clear()
instead copies the data out while retaining the original buffer's
capacity, avoiding repeated alloc/dealloc churn in the stdin reader
(rpc.rs) and markdown code-block splitter (tui.rs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
